description: Add an Agent Spec generation workflow to the VS Code extension so a user can generate an implementation-ready task specification for a task stored in .agent-board/tasks/. The feature should use existing project context from .agent-board/project.json and the selected task JSON, populate the task's description, acceptanceCriteria, qaChecklist, designQaChecklist, validationCommands, and constraints fields, and preserve all unknown fields in the task file. The generated spec must be concrete enough for an AI coding agent to claim and implement without relying on copied prompts.
acceptanceCriteria: A user can trigger Agent Spec generation for an existing task from the VS Code extension UI or command surface.
The implementation reads .agent-board/project.json and the selected .agent-board/tasks/TASK-ID.json before generating the spec.

The generated spec updates only the intended task fields: description, acceptanceCriteria, qaChecklist, designQaChecklist, validationCommands, and constraints, plus lastUpdated if that pattern already exists in task updates.
Unknown fields in the task JSON are preserved exactly when writing the updated task file.
If project validation commands exist, the generated validationCommands field includes the relevant commands, with npm run compile included at minimum for this repository.
The workflow handles missing or malformed project/task JSON with a clear VS Code error message and does not write partial task updates.
The board UI refreshes after successful generation so the updated task details are visible without restarting VS Code.
No real Claude, Codex, or external API calls are introduced; generation should use the repository's existing local/spec-building patterns or a deterministic local implementation.
qaChecklist: Create or use a task with empty spec fields and run the Agent Spec generation workflow.
Verify .agent-board/project.json context is reflected in the generated task spec.
Verify the task JSON contains populated description, acceptanceCriteria, qaChecklist, designQaChecklist, validationCommands, and constraints arrays after generation.
Add an extra unknown field to the task JSON, run generation, and confirm the field remains unchanged.
Run generation against a missing task file and confirm a clear error appears with no new file written.
Run generation against malformed JSON and confirm the extension reports the failure without corrupting the file.
Confirm the board refreshes and displays the generated spec after success.
designQaChecklist: Confirm the generation action is easy to discover from the task details or task controls area.
Confirm the UI remains dense and operational, without adding marketing-style explanatory sections.
Confirm success and failure states are visible without requiring repeated manual clicks.
Confirm long generated descriptions and checklist items remain readable in the task detail view without layout overlap or clipped controls.
constraints: Follow existing TypeScript and React patterns in this repository.
Keep extension backend logic in src/ and webview UI logic in webview/.
Use .agent-board/project.json and the task JSON as the source of truth.
Preserve unknown fields in Agent Board JSON files when writing updates.
Do not introduce real Claude, Codex, OpenAI, or other external API calls for this task.
Keep the implementation scoped to Agent Spec generation and display refresh behavior only.
